title: Steam Co-Review Network (82K Games)
subtitle: 82K games connected by 128M shared user reviews
description:
# Steam Co-Review Network

Two Steam games share an edge when multiple users reviewed both. Built from 128 million user reviews across 80,000 games (2012-2024), this dataset maps how the Steam catalog is connected through player overlap.

## What's Inside

### steam_network_full.json (1.4 GB)
The complete co-review graph — 48,362 nodes, 33 million weighted edges:
- Every game with 10+ reviews included as a node
- Edges kept when 2+ users reviewed both games
- No per-node neighbor cap — full topology preserved
- Edge weights range from 2 to 420,410 shared reviewers

### steam_all_2005.json (game catalog)
82,928 games released 2005-2025 with:
- Title, release year, positive review ratio, total review count
- Price, developer, rating category
- Genre indices and tag indices (referencing lookup tables in the file)

Packed as arrays for compact JSON: `[name, year, ratio, reviews, price, ratingIdx, genreIdxs, tagIdxs, developer]`

### steam_force_layout.json (precomputed layout)
Fruchterman-Reingold positions for the top ~9K nodes. Use as warm-start coordinates for force-directed visualization.

## Sources

- **Game metadata**: [FronkonGames Steam Games Dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/FronkonGames/steam-games-dataset) (Jan 2026 snapshot, 122K games)
- **User reviews**: [artermiloff Steam Reviews 2024](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/artermiloff/steam-games-reviews-2024) (128M reviews across 80K games, one CSV per game, 2012-June 2024)

## How It Was Built

1. Load game metadata from FronkonGames enriched CSV
2. Scan 30K+ per-game review CSVs, extract user-game pairs
3. For each user who reviewed 2+ games, generate all game pairs
4. Count shared reviewers per pair to get edge weights
5. Filter by minimum shared reviewers

Full pipeline code: [github.com/lukeslp/steam-game-network](https://github.com/lukeslp/steam-game-network)

## Live Visualization

[dr.eamer.dev/datavis/interactive/steam-network/](https://dr.eamer.dev/datavis/interactive/steam-network/)

Six interactive views: scatter plot, chord diagram, force-directed network, streamgraph, heatmap, and treemap. All Canvas-rendered for performance at 80K+ data points.
